Source_Species.reason: CamTPS7 is explicitly described as a gene from Callicarpa americana.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: CamTPS7 is described as a class II diTPS; class II diTPSs are placed in TPS-c in the phylogenetic analysis.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The Results state 'CamTPS7 was confirmed to be a (+)-CPP synthase'.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number is given for CamTPS7 in the paper.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: CamTPS7 is part of the C. americana BGC discussed as a miltiradiene/(+)-kaurene diterpene backbone pathway within abietane-related diterpenoid biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: CamTPS7 is described as another predicted/confirmed (+)-CPP synthase in the second half of the cluster, supplying precursor for that module.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: As a class II (+)-CPP synthase acting before class I diTPSs, CamTPS7 functions upstream.

Primary_Substrate.reason: By definition of class II diterpene synthases in this paper, (+)-CPP synthases cyclize GGPP; the pathway description supports GGPP as substrate.

Primary_Product.reason: CamTPS7 was experimentally confirmed to make (+)-CPP.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: This accurately summarizes the confirmed role of CamTPS7 as a class II diTPS producing (+)-CPP for downstream diterpene formation.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: CamTPS7 supplies the precursor used by downstream enzymes including CamTPS10.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: CamTPS7 supplies (+)-CPP used for CamTPS9-derived miltiradiene and CamTPS10-derived (+)-kaurene-related diterpenes.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper discusses ecological/defense roles of Lamiaceae specialized diterpenes and plant growth-promoting potential of calliterpenone, but does not directly establish these functions for CamTPS7 products in C. americana.
